title: South Asia
subtitle: Stupas, cave temples, bronze gods, and marble mausoleums.
intro: Hindu, Buddhist, Jain, and Islamic traditions layered on top of each other for two millennia. Much of the greatest work is architecture carved straight out of living rock.

works.title: Mohenjo-daro
works.meta: Indus Valley Civilization · c. 2500 BCE · Pakistan
works.desc: A Bronze Age city with grid streets, covered drains, and a Great Bath. Its script is still undeciphered.

works.title: Great Stupa at Sanchi
works.desc: A hemispherical mound containing relics of the Buddha. The four carved gateways around it narrate his life without ever depicting him directly.

works.title: Gandhara Buddha
works.desc: The first sculptural images of the Buddha, made in schist and draped in togas. A crossroads style born from Alexander's successors.

works.title: Ajanta Caves
works.meta: Buddhist · 2nd c. BCE – 6th c. CE · Maharashtra
works.desc: Thirty rock-cut monasteries with surviving murals of the Buddha's past lives. Abandoned, forgotten, and rediscovered by a tiger-hunting British officer in 1819.

works.title: Nataraja (Shiva as Lord of Dance)
works.desc: Shiva dances the universe into being and out of it again, surrounded by a ring of flame, trampling the demon of ignorance.

works.title: Brihadeeswarar Temple
works.desc: A granite tower 216 feet high, capped with a single 80-ton stone. A thousand years old and still in daily ritual use.

works.title: Khajuraho Temples
works.desc: Hindu and Jain temples covered in dense sculpture — gods, battles, and the frank erotic scenes the site is best known for.

works.title: Konark Sun Temple
works.desc: A massive stone chariot for the sun god, pulled by seven horses, wheels carved to tell the time from their shadows.

works.title: Hampi (Vijayanagara)
works.desc: Capital of the last great Hindu empire in the south. Sacked in 1565; the boulder-strewn ruins sprawl over 16 square miles.

works.title: Meenakshi Temple
works.desc: Fourteen gateway towers covered in thousands of painted figures. The whole complex is repainted every twelve years.

works.title: Taj Mahal
works.desc: A tomb Shah Jahan built for his wife Mumtaz Mahal, who died in childbirth. The marble is inlaid with 28 kinds of gem and stone.
